revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 'トベ（戸畔、戸辨、戸辺、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は原始的カバネの一つで、女性首長の名称に使われた。後に一般的...'
revision.text: トベ（戸畔、戸辨、戸辺、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は原始的カバネの一つで、女性首長の名称に使われた。後に一般的姓や地名として使われる。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女）やトジ（[[刀自]]）の語源でもある。<ref 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年> ==ヤマト王権以前== [[神武天皇]]紀にヤマト王権に服従しない名草戸畔（[[紀伊国]]）、丹敷戸畔（[[熊野国]]）、および新城戸畔（[[大和国]]）の女性首長の名が残されている。作鏡連の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（石凝姥）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。原始的な「トベ」から「トメ」への変化をここから読み取ることができる。 ==初期ヤマト王権期== 木（紀伊） 国造、荒河戸辨の子女は[[崇神天皇]]の妃となり、[[豊城入彦]]（上毛野氏および下毛野氏の祖）を生む。同時代に氷香戸邊（丹波国氷上）、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃に苅幡戸邊およびに綺戸邊（[[山代国]]）が見える。 ==地名のトベ== 現在、戸部町（横浜市）や戸部川（神奈川県）がある。かつて尾張国愛智郡には戸部村が存在した。伊予国伊予郡には砥部村があった。信濃国斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた。トメがトベからの言い換えであったことがここから読み取れる。<ref 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、吉田東吾『地名大辞典」＞ ==人名のトベ== 現在、戸辺姓は千葉県および関東の利根川沿いに集中している。戸部姓は群馬県に集中している。富部姓は静岡県に集中している。土部姓は神奈川県と富山県に集中している。<ref「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査＞ == 脚注 == <references/>

トベ（戸畔、戸辨、戸辺、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は原始的カバネの一つで、女性首長の名称に使われた。後に一般的姓や地名として使われる。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女）やトジ（[[刀自]]）の語源でもある。<ref> 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年</ref>
==ヤマト王権以前== [[神武天皇]]紀にヤマト王権に服従しない名草戸畔（[[紀伊国]]）、丹敷戸畔（[[熊野国]]）、および新城戸畔（[[大和国]]）の女性首長の名が残されている。作鏡連の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（石凝姥）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。原始的な「トベ」から「トメ」への変化をここから読み取ることができる。
==初期ヤマト王権期== 木（紀伊） 国造、荒河戸辨の子女は[[崇神天皇]]の妃となり、[[豊城入彦]]（上毛野氏および下毛野氏の祖）を生む。同時代に氷香戸邊（丹波国氷上）、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃に苅幡戸邊およびに綺戸邊（[[山代国]]）が見える。
==地名のトベ== 現在、戸部町（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛智郡]]には戸部村が存在した。[[伊予国]]伊予郡には砥部村があった。[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた。トメがトベからの言い換えであったことがここから読み取れる。<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、吉田東伍『地名大辞典」</ref>
==人名のトベ== 現在、戸辺姓は[[千葉県]]および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している。<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>
== 脚注 == <references/>

==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛智郡]]には戸部村が存在した。[[伊予国]]伊予郡には砥部村があった。[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた。トメがトベからの言い換えであったことがここから読み取れる。<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>
==人名のトベ== 現在、戸辺姓は[[千葉県]]および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している。<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>
== 脚注 == <references/> [[Category:歴史]]

トベ（戸畔、戸弁、戸辨、戸辺、戸邊、刀俾、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は[[ヤマト王権]]以前の称号（原始的[[カバネ]]）の一つで、4世紀以前の女性首長の名称に使われた。後に一般的姓や地名として使われる。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女、刀咩）の語源でもある。<ref> 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年</ref>
==女性首長== [[神武天皇]]紀にヤマト王権に服従しない[[紀ノ川]]河口のナグサトベ（[[名草戸畔]]）、[[熊野]]地方のニシキトベ（丹敷戸畔）、および[[大和国]]層富県（そほのあがた） のニキトベ（新城戸畔）という女性首長の名が残されている。 ヤマト王権の初期には木国造、すなわち紀伊の首長であるアラカワトベ（荒河戸辨）は子女を[[崇神天皇]]の妃におくり、[[豊城入彦命]]（上毛野氏および下毛野氏の祖）や[[豊鍬入姫命]]が生まれている。同時代に丹波国氷上郡の女性首長にヒカトベ（氷香戸邊）、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃に苅幡戸邊およびに[[山代国]]のカニハタトベ（綺戸邊）が見える。[[ヤマトタケル]]の東征に仕えた[[尾張国]]のイナダネ（[[建稲種命]]）は母にマシキトベ（眞敷刀俾、[[下知我麻神社]]の祭神）、子にシリツナマワカトベ（尾綱真若刀俾）、別名シリツキトメ（志理都紀斗売）を伝えている。 ==トベからトメへ== 作鏡連の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（石凝姥）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。元はイシコリ「トベ」と呼ばれていたのが、後にイシコリ「ドメ」に変えられた事をここから知ることができる。[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>。したがって信濃国の神社に見られる、ヒカナトメ（氷鉋斗賣）神社、サラシナトメ（更級斗女）神社、カワナカジマトメ（川中島斗賣）神社および祭神にヤサカトメ（八坂刀売）は元来はトベと呼ばれていたことが推測される。
==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]]伊予郡には砥部村があった。 ==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺姓は[[千葉県]]および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している。<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>

'''トベ'''（戸畔、戸弁、戸辨、戸辺、戸邊、戸邉、刀俾、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は[[ヤマト王権]]以前の称号（原始的[[カバネ]]）の一つで、4世紀以前の女性首長の名称に使われた。後に一般的姓や地名として使われる。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女、刀咩）の語源でもある。<ref> 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年</ref>
==女性首長== [[神武天皇]]紀にヤマト王権に服従しない[[紀ノ川]]河口のナグサトベ（[[名草戸畔]]）、[[熊野]]地方のニシキトベ（丹敷戸畔）、および[[大和国]]層富県（そほのあがた） のニキトベ（新城戸畔）という女性首長の名が残されている。 ヤマト王権の初期には木国造、すなわち紀伊の首長であるアラカワトベ（荒河戸辨）は子女を[[崇神天皇]]の妃におくり、[[豊城入彦命]]（上毛野氏および下毛野氏の祖）や[[豊鍬入姫命]]が生まれている。同時代に丹波国氷上郡の女性首長にヒカトベ（氷香戸邊）、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃に苅幡戸邊およびに[[山代国]]のカニハタトベ（綺戸邊）が見える。[[ヤマトタケル]]の東征に仕えた[[尾張国]]のイナダネ（[[建稲種命]]）は母にマシキトベ（眞敷刀俾、[[下知我麻神社]]の祭神）、子にシリツナマワカトベ（尾綱真若刀俾）、別名シリツキトメ（志理都紀斗売）を伝えている。
==トベからトメへ== 作鏡連の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（石凝姥）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。元はイシコリ「トベ」と呼ばれていたのが、後にイシコリ「ドメ」に変えられた事をここから知ることができる。[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>。したがって信濃国の神社に見られる、ヒカナトメ（氷鉋斗賣）神社、サラシナトメ（更級斗女）神社、カワナカジマトメ（川中島斗賣）神社および祭神にヤサカトメ（八坂刀売）は元来はトベと呼ばれていたことが推測される。
==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]][[伊予郡]]には砥部村→[[砥部町]]がある。
==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している。<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 外部リンク == + [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm 戸畔考]

==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]][[伊予郡]]には砥部村→[[砥部町]]がある。
==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している。<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>

'''トベ'''（戸畔、戸弁、戸辨、戸辺、戸邊、戸邉、刀俾、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は[[ヤマト王権]]以前の称号（原始的[[カバネ]]）の一つで、4世紀以前の女性首長の名称に使われた。後に一般的姓や地名として使われる。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女、刀咩）の語源でもある。<ref> 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年</ref>
==女性首長== [[神武天皇]]紀にヤマト王権に服従しない[[紀ノ川]]河口のナグサトベ（[[名草戸畔]]）、[[熊野]]地方のニシキトベ（丹敷戸畔）、および[[大和国]]層富県（そほのあがた） のニキトベ（新城戸畔）という女性首長の名が残されている。 ヤマト王権の初期には木国造、すなわち紀伊の首長であるアラカワトベ（荒河戸辨）は子女を[[崇神天皇]]の妃におくり、[[豊城入彦命]]（上毛野氏および下毛野氏の祖）や[[豊鍬入姫命]]が生まれている。同時代に[[丹波国]][[氷上郡]]の女性首長にヒカトベ（氷香戸邊）、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃にカリハタトベ（苅幡戸邊）およびに[[山代国]]のカニハタトベ（綺戸邊）が見える。[[ヤマトタケル]]の東征に仕えた[[尾張国]]のイナダネ（[[建稲種命]]）は母にマシキトベ（眞敷刀俾、[[下知我麻神社]]の祭神）、子にシリツナマワカトベ（尾綱真若刀俾）、別名シリツキトメ（志理都紀斗売）を伝えている。
==トベとネ（トネ）== トベの称号でよばれた女性首長は「ネ」の称号で呼ばれた男性首長としばしば対をなしている。先代旧事本紀の尾張氏系図には「 オツナネ」(尾綱根)と「オツナマワカトベ」（尾綱真若刀俾）を兄妹の関係で伝えている。また[[海部氏系図]]ではシリツキトベ（志里都岐刀邊）がシリツキネ（志里都岐根）と親族関係で書かれている。大和国の葛城縣の長に「ツルギネ（剣根）」が伝えられているが、その系図の一つに「ヤマトベ（夜麻都俾）」が剣根の次に伝えられている。「トベ」が女性首長の称号なら、ここでも男性首長の「ネ」と対になっていると見ることが出来る。さらに「トベ」と「トネ」が対となった地方首長を推測させるものも残されている。「アラカワトベ（荒河戸辨）」は木国の女首長（キトベ）であるが、木国造の系図には始祖に「キトネ（鬼刀禰）」と伝えているものが存在する。ニキトベ（新城戸畔）は大和国の六御県の一つ層富県の女性首長と伝えられているが、大和国の六御県には「[[トネ（刀禰)|刀禰]]」という男性の長が知られている。もし「トベ」と「トネ」が男女の対の長を意味する称号であるなら、古代[[ヒメヒコ制]]の名残と考えられる。
==トベからトメへ== 作鏡連の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（石凝姥）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。海部氏系図に見られるシリツキトベ（志里都岐刀邊）は古事記ではシリツキトメ（志理都紀斗賣）となっている。この二つの例から「トベ」という尊称が、後に「トメ」という尊称に変えられた事を知ることができる。また[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>。したがって信濃国の神社に見られる、ヒカナトメ（氷鉋斗賣）神社、サラシナトメ（更級斗女）神社、カワナカジマトメ（川中島斗賣）神社および諏訪神社祭神のヤサカトメ（八坂刀売）は元来は「トベ」語尾をもって呼ばれていたことが推測される。また[[先代旧事本紀]]や海部氏系図に現れるアマトメ（天登目命、天斗米命）、タケトメ（建登米命、建斗米命）、タエトメ（妙斗米命）の子孫にムトベ（ 六戸部、六人部、身人部）連が知られており、ここでもトベとトメの関連が見出される。
==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]][[伊予郡]]には砥部村→[[砥部町]]がある。
==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している。<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 外部リンク == + [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm 戸畔考]

'''トベ'''（戸畔、戸弁、戸辨、戸辺、戸邊、戸邉、刀俾、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は[[ヤマト王権]]以前の称号（原始的[[カバネ]]）の一つで、4世紀以前の女性首長の名称に使われた。後に一般的姓や地名として使われる。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女、刀咩）の語源でもある。<ref> 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年</ref>
==女性首長== [[神武天皇]]紀にヤマト王権に服従しない[[紀ノ川]]河口のナグサトベ（[[名草戸畔]]）、[[熊野]]地方のニシキトベ（丹敷戸畔）、および[[大和国]]層富県（そほのあがた） のニキトベ（新城戸畔）という女性首長の名が残されている。 ヤマト王権の初期には木国造、すなわち紀伊の首長であるアラカワトベ（荒河戸辨）は子女を[[崇神天皇]]の妃におくり、[[豊城入彦命]]（上毛野氏および下毛野氏の祖）や[[豊鍬入姫命]]が生まれている。同時代に[[丹波国]][[氷上郡]]の女性首長にヒカトベ（氷香戸邊）、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃にカリハタトベ（苅幡戸邊）およびに[[山代国]]のカニハタトベ（綺戸邊）が見える。[[ヤマトタケル]]の東征に仕えた[[尾張国]]のイナダネ（[[建稲種命]]）は母にマシキトベ（眞敷刀俾、[[下知我麻神社]]の祭神）、子にシリツナマワカトベ（尾綱真若刀俾）、別名シリツキトメ（志理都紀斗売）を伝えている。
トベの称号でよばれた女性首長は「[[ネ (根、禰)|ネ]]」の称号で呼ばれた男性首長としばしば対をなしている。先代旧事本紀の尾張氏系図には「 オツナネ」(尾綱根)と「オツナマワカトベ」（尾綱真若刀俾）を兄妹の関係で伝えている。また[[海部氏系図]]ではシリツキトベ（志里都岐刀邊）がシリツキネ（志里都岐根）と親族関係で書かれている。大和国の葛城縣の長に「ツルギネ（剣根）」が伝えられているが、その系図の一つに「ヤマトベ（夜麻都俾）」が剣根の次に伝えられている。「トベ」が女性首長の称号なら、ここでも男性首長の「ネ」と対になっていると見ることが出来る。さらに「トベ」と「トネ」が対となった地方首長を推測させるものも残されている。「アラカワトベ（荒河戸辨）」は木国の女首長（キトベ）であるが、木国造の系図には始祖に「キトネ（鬼刀禰）」と伝えているものが存在する。ニキトベ（新城戸畔）は大和国の六御県の一つ層富県の女性首長と伝えられているが、大和国の六御県には「[[刀禰|トネ（刀禰)]]」という男性の長が知られている。もし「トベ」と「トネ」が男女の対の長を意味する称号であるなら、古代[[ヒメヒコ制]]の名残と考えられる。
==トベからトメへ== 作鏡連の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（石凝姥）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。海部氏系図に見られるシリツキトベ（志里都岐刀邊）は古事記ではシリツキトメ（志理都紀斗賣）となっている。この二つの例から「トベ」という尊称が、後に「トメ」という尊称に変えられた事を知ることができる。また[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>。したがって信濃国の神社に見られる、ヒカナトメ（氷鉋斗賣）神社、サラシナトメ（更級斗女）神社、カワナカジマトメ（川中島斗賣）神社および諏訪神社祭神のヤサカトメ（八坂刀売）は元来は「トベ」語尾をもって呼ばれていたことが推測される。また[[先代旧事本紀]]や海部氏系図に現れるアマトメ（天登目命、天斗米命）、タケトメ（建登米命、建斗米命）、タエトメ（妙斗米命）の子孫にムトベ（ 六戸部、六人部、身人部）連が知られており、ここでもトベとトメの関連が見出される。
==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]][[伊予郡]]には砥部村→[[砥部町]]がある。
==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している。<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 外部リンク == + [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm 戸畔考]

'''トベ'''（戸畔、戸弁、戸辨、戸辺、戸邊、戸邉、刀俾、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は[[ヤマト王権]]以前の称号（原始的[[カバネ]]）の一つで、4世紀以前の女性首長の名称に使われた。後に一般的姓や地名として使われる。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女、刀咩）の語源でもある。<ref> 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年</ref>

==トベからトメへ== 作鏡連の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（石凝姥）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。海部氏系図に見られるシリツキトベ（志里都岐刀邊）は古事記ではシリツキトメ（志理都紀斗賣）となっている。この二つの例から「トベ」という尊称が、後に「トメ」という尊称に変えられた事を知ることができる。また[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>。したがって信濃国の神社に見られる、ヒカナトメ（氷鉋斗賣）神社、サラシナトメ（更級斗女）神社、カワナカジマトメ（川中島斗賣）神社および諏訪神社祭神のヤサカトメ（八坂刀売）は元来は「トベ」語尾をもって呼ばれていたことが推測される。また[[先代旧事本紀]]や海部氏系図に現れるアマトメ（天登目命、天斗米命）、タケトメ（建登米命、建斗米命）、タエトメ（妙斗米命）の子孫にムトベ（ 六戸部、六人部、身人部）連が知られており、ここでもトベとトメの関連が見出される。
==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]][[伊予郡]]には砥部村→[[砥部町]]がある。
==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している。<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm 戸畔考]

==トベからトメへ== 作鏡連の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（石凝姥）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。海部氏系図に見られるシリツキトベ（志里都岐刀邊）は古事記ではシリツキトメ（志理都紀斗賣）となっている。この二つの例から「トベ」という尊称が、後に「トメ」という尊称に変えられた事を知ることができる。また[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>。したがって信濃国の神社に見られる、ヒカナトメ（氷鉋斗賣）神社、サラシナトメ（更級斗女）神社、カワナカジマトメ（川中島斗賣）神社および諏訪神社祭神のヤサカトメ（八坂刀売）は元来は「トベ」語尾をもって呼ばれていたことが推測される。また[[先代旧事本紀]]や海部氏系図に現れるアマトメ（天登目命、天斗米命）、タケトメ（建登米命、建斗米命）、タエトメ（妙斗米命）の子孫にムトベ（ 六戸部、六人部、身人部）連が知られており、ここでもトベとトメの関連が見出される。
==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]][[伊予郡]]には砥部村→[[砥部町]]がある。
==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>。

== 外部リンク == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm 戸畔考]

'''トベ'''（戸畔、戸弁、戸辨、戸辺、戸邊、戸邉、刀俾、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は、[[ヤマト王権]]以前の称号（原始的[[カバネ]]）の一つで、4世紀以前の女性首長の名称に使われた。後に一般的姓や地名として使われる。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女、刀咩）の語源でもある<ref> 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年</ref>。

==トベからトメへ== 作鏡連の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（石凝姥）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。海部氏系図に見られるシリツキトベ（志里都岐刀邊）は古事記ではシリツキトメ（志理都紀斗賣）となっている。この二つの例から「トベ」という尊称が、後に「トメ」という尊称に変えられた事を知ることができる。また[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>。したがって信濃国の神社に見られる、ヒカナトメ（氷鉋斗賣）神社、サラシナトメ（更級斗女）神社、カワナカジマトメ（川中島斗賣）神社および[[諏訪大社|諏訪神社]]祭神のヤサカトメ（[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売]]）は元来は「トベ」語尾をもって呼ばれていたことが推測される。また[[先代旧事本紀]]や海部氏系図に現れるアマトメ（天登目命、天斗米命）、タケトメ（建登米命、建斗米命）、タエトメ（妙斗米命）の子孫にムトベ（ 六戸部、六人部、身人部）連が知られており、ここでもトベとトメの関連が見出される。
==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]][[伊予郡]]には砥部村→[[砥部町]]がある。
==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>。

{{デフォルトソート:とへ}} [[Category:古代日本の称号]] [[Category:古語]]

'''トベ'''（戸畔、戸弁、戸辨、戸辺、戸邊、戸邉、刀俾、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は、[[ヤマト王権]]以前の称号（原始的[[カバネ]]）の一つで、4世紀以前の女性首長の名称に使われた。後に一般的姓や地名として使われる。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女、刀咩）の語源でもある<ref> 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年</ref>。
==女性首長== [[神武天皇]]紀にヤマト王権に服従しない[[紀ノ川]]河口のナグサトベ（[[名草戸畔]]）、[[熊野]]地方のニシキトベ（丹敷戸畔）、および[[大和国]]層富県（そほのあがた） のニキトベ（新城戸畔）という女性首長の名が残されている。 ヤマト王権の初期には木国造、すなわち紀伊の首長であるアラカワトベ（荒河戸辨）は子女を[[崇神天皇]]の妃におくり、[[豊城入彦命]]（上毛野氏および下毛野氏の祖）や[[豊鍬入姫命]]が生まれている。同時代に[[丹波国]][[氷上郡]]の女性首長にヒカトベ（氷香戸邊）、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃にカリハタトベ（苅幡戸邊）およびに[[山代国]]のカニハタトベ（綺戸邊）が見える。[[ヤマトタケル]]の東征に仕えた[[尾張国]]のイナダネ（[[建稲種命]]）は母にマシキトベ（眞敷刀俾、[[下知我麻神社]]の祭神）、子にオツナマワカトベ（尾綱真若刀俾）、別名シリツキトメ（志理都紀斗売）を伝えている。
== トベとトメ == [[作鏡連]]の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（伊斯許理度売命、石凝姥命）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。『[[古事記]]』に見られるシリツキトメ（志理都紀斗賣）は、『[[海部氏系図]]』{{efn|偽書とする説がある。}}ではシリツキトベ（志里都岐刀邊）となっている。
[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>。また信濃国にはヒカナトメ（[[氷鉋斗賣神社]]）、サラシナトメ（[[更級斗女神社]]）、カワナカジマトメ（[[川中島斗賣神社]]）、[[八坂刀売神|ヤサカトメ]]（[[諏訪大社]]下社）と神名に「トメ」がつく神が多く祀られている。
また『[[先代旧事本紀]]』や『海部氏系図』に現れるアマトメ（天登目命、天斗米命）、タケトメ（建登米命、建斗米命）、タエトメ（妙斗米命）の子孫にムトベ（ 六戸部、六人部、身人部）連が知られており、ここでもトベとトメの関連が見出される。ただし「刀売、斗売、戸弁、戸辺」が[[上代特殊仮名遣い]]の「ト」甲類と「メ・ベ」甲類であるのに対して、「登米・登目」は「ト」乙類と「メ」乙類であり、「斗米」は「ト」甲類と「メ」乙類であるため、首長を表す「トベ・トメ」とは全くの別物である。
==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]][[伊予郡]]には砥部村→[[砥部町]]がある。
==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>。

==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm 戸畔考]
{{デフォルトソート:とへ}} [[Category:古代日本の称号]] [[Category:古語]]

'''トベ'''（戸畔、戸弁、戸辨、戸辺、戸邊、戸邉、刀俾、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は、[[ヤマト王権]]以前の称号（原始的[[カバネ]]）の一つで、{{要出典範囲|後に一般的姓や地名として使われる|date=2020年7月}}。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女、刀咩）の語源でもある<ref> 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年</ref>。
==首長== [[神武天皇]]紀にヤマト王権に服従しない[[紀ノ川]]河口のナグサトベ（[[名草戸畔]]）、[[熊野]]地方のニシキトベ（丹敷戸畔）、および[[大和国]]層富県（そほのあがた） のニキトベ（新城戸畔）という首長の名が残されている。 ヤマト王権の初期には木国造、すなわち紀伊の首長であるアラカワトベ（[[荒河戸畔|荒河戸辨命]]）は子女を[[崇神天皇]]の妃におくり、[[豊城入彦命]]（上毛野氏および下毛野氏の祖）や[[豊鍬入姫命]]が生まれている。同時代に[[丹波国]][[氷上郡]]の女性首長にヒカトベ（氷香戸邊）、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃に[[山背国造]]の娘・カリハタトベ（苅幡戸邊）およびにカニハタトベ（綺戸邊）が見える。[[倭建命]]の東征に仕えた[[尾張国造]]族の[[建稲種命]]は母にマシキトベ（眞敷刀俾、[[下知我麻神社]]の祭神）、子にオツナマワカトベ（尾綱真若刀俾）、別名シリツキトメ（志理都紀斗売）を伝えている。
== トベとトメ == [[作鏡連]]の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（伊斯許理度売命、石凝姥命）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。『[[古事記]]』に見られるシリツキトメ（志理都紀斗賣）は、『[[海部氏系図]]』{{efn|偽書とする説がある。}}ではシリツキトベ（志里都岐刀邊）となっている。
[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>。また信濃国にはヒカナトメ（[[氷鉋斗賣神社]]）、サラシナトメ（[[更級斗女神社]]）、カワナカジマトメ（[[川中島斗賣神社]]）、[[八坂刀売神|ヤサカトメ]]（[[諏訪大社]]下社）と神名に「トメ」がつく神が多く祀られている。
また『[[先代旧事本紀]]』や『海部氏系図』に現れるアマトメ（天登目命、天斗米命）、タケトメ（建登米命、建斗米命）、タエトメ（妙斗米命）の子孫にムトベ（ 六戸部、六人部、身人部）連が知られており、ここでもトベとトメの関連が見出されるとする説がある。ただし「刀売、斗売、戸弁、戸辺」が[[上代特殊仮名遣い]]の「ト」甲類と「メ・ベ」甲類であるのに対して、「登米・登目」は「ト」乙類と「メ」乙類であり、「斗米」は「ト」甲類と「メ」乙類であるため、首長を表す「トベ・トメ」とは全くの別物である。また六人部の部は[[部民制|部]]の意味であり、六人部も「六」+「人部」ではなく「六人」+「部」である。
==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]][[伊予郡]]には砥部村→[[砥部町]]がある。
==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>。

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 19..."
revision.text:
'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kino River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of {{ill|Ki Province|ja|紀国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokuniirihime-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Kenu clans|ja|上毛野氏 and 下毛野氏}}) and {{ill|Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeru Inatane-no-mikoto|ja|建稲種命}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]
[[Category:Ancient Japanese Titles]] [[Category:Old Japanese Words]] [[ja:トベ]]

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>

Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).

Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

revision.text:
'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kino River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of {{ill|Ki Province|ja|紀国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokuniirihime-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Kenu clans|ja|上毛野氏 and 下毛野氏}}) and {{ill|Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeru Inatane-no-mikoto|ja|建稲種命}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kino River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of {{ill|Ki Province|ja|紀国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokuniirihime-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Kenu clans|ja|上毛野氏 and 下毛野氏}}) and {{ill|Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeru Inatane-no-mikoto|ja|建稲種命}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kino River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of {{ill|Ki Province|ja|紀国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokuniirihime-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Kenu clans|ja|上毛野氏 and 下毛野氏}}) and {{ill|Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeru Inatane-no-mikoto|ja|建稲種命}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).

Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).

Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."

In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kino River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of {{ill|Ki Province|ja|紀国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokuniirihime-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Kenu clans|ja|上毛野氏 and 下毛野氏}}) and {{ill|Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeru Inatane-no-mikoto|ja|建稲種命}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of {{ill|Ki Province|ja|紀国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokuniirihime-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

revision.text:
'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.

== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tobe-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tobe-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tobe-qq]] [[Category:Old Japanese Words]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tobe]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tobe]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[ja:トベ]]

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tobe-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tobe-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tobe-qq]] [[Category:Old Japanese Words]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tobe]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tobe]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:トベ]]

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tobe-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tobe-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tobe-qq]] [[Category:Old Japanese Words]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tobe]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tobe]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:トベ]]

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).

Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).

Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."

In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Tobe—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Tobe—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--Tobe—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Old Japanese Words]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tobe]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tobe]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:トベ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tobe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tobe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tobe]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
