'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

revision.text:
'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."

In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|日本武尊}}" to "|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}"
revision.text:
'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

revision.text:
'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=draft}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=draft}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=draft}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)|12=draft}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国|12=draft}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町|12=draft}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市|12=draft}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=draft}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野|12=draft}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|12=draft}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国|12=draft}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=draft}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).

Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)|12=draft}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国|12=draft}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町|12=draft}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市|12=draft}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=draft}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野|12=draft}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|12=draft}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国|12=draft}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=draft}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=draft}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=draft}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=draft}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)|12=draft}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国|12=draft}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町|12=draft}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市|12=draft}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).

Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=draft|12=draft}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=draft|12=draft}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft|12=draft}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市|12=draft|12=draft}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:S needing substitution checking]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft|12=draft}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=draft|12=draft}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=draft|12=draft}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=draft|12=draft}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=draft|12=draft}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft|12=draft}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市|12=draft|12=draft}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

revision.comment: 34 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:トベ up to rev 78810914
revision.text:
'''トベ'''（戸畔、戸弁、戸辨、戸辺、戸邊、戸邉、刀俾、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は、[[ヤマト王権]]以前の称号（原始的[[カバネ]]）の一つで、{{要出典範囲|後に一般的姓や地名として使われる|date=2020年7月}}。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女、刀咩）の語源でもある<ref> 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年</ref>。
==首長== 『[[日本書紀]]』の[[神武天皇]]紀には、天皇に服従しない[[紀ノ川]]河口の[[名草戸畔]]（ナグサトベ）、[[熊野]]地方の丹敷戸畔（ニシキトベ）、および[[大和国]]層富県（そほのあがた） の新城戸畔（ニキトベ）という首長の名が残されている。
[[ヤマト王権]]の初期には[[紀国造|木国造]]、すなわち紀伊の首長である[[荒河戸畔|荒河戸辨命]]（アラカワトベ）は子女を[[崇神天皇]]の妃におくり、[[豊城入彦命]]（[[上毛野氏]]および[[下毛野氏]]の祖）や[[豊鍬入姫命]]が生まれている。同時代に[[丹波国]][[氷上郡]]の首長に[[氷香戸辺]]（ヒカトベ）、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃に[[山背国造]]の娘・苅幡戸邊（カリハタトベ）およびに綺戸邊（カニハタトベ）が見える。[[倭建命]]の東征に仕えた[[尾張国造]]の[[建稲種命]]は母に眞敷刀俾（マシキトベ、[[下知我麻神社]]の祭神）、子に尾綱真若刀俾命（オツナマワカトベ）、別名を志理都紀斗売（シリツキトメ）と伝えている。
== トベとトメ == [[作鏡連]]の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（伊斯許理度売命、石凝姥命）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。『[[古事記]]』に見られるシリツキトメ（志理都紀斗賣）は、『[[海部氏系図]]』{{efn|偽書とする説がある。}}ではシリツキトベ（志里都岐刀邊）となっている。
[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>。また信濃国にはヒカナトメ（[[氷鉋斗賣神社]]）、サラシナトメ（[[更級斗女神社]]）、カワナカジマトメ（[[川中島斗賣神社]]）、[[八坂刀売神|ヤサカトメ]]（[[諏訪大社]]下社）と神名に「トメ」がつく神が多く祀られている。
また『[[先代旧事本紀]]』や『海部氏系図』に現れるアマトメ（天登目命、天斗米命）、タケトメ（建登米命、建斗米命）、タエトメ（妙斗米命）の子孫にムトベ（ 六戸部、六人部、身人部）連が知られており、ここでもトベとトメの関連が見出されるとする説がある。ただし「刀売、斗売、戸弁、戸辺」が[[上代特殊仮名遣い]]の「ト」甲類と「メ・ベ」甲類であるのに対して、「登米・登目」は「ト」乙類と「メ」乙類であり、「斗米」は「ト」甲類と「メ」乙類であるため、首長を表す「トベ・トメ」とは全くの別物である。また六人部の部は[[部民制|部]]の意味であり、六人部も「六」+「人部」ではなく「六人」+「部」である。
==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]][[伊予郡]]には砥部村→[[砥部町]]がある。
==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm 戸畔考]
{{デフォルトソート:とへ}} [[Category:古代日本の称号]] [[Category:古語]]

'''トベ'''（戸畔、戸弁、戸辨、戸辺、戸邊、戸邉、刀俾、戸部、富部、砥部、土部）は、[[ヤマト王権]]以前の称号（原始的[[カバネ]]）の一つで、{{要出典範囲|後に一般的姓や地名として使われる|date=2020年7月}}。トベはトメ（戸賣、斗女、刀咩）の語源でもある<ref> 太田亮『日本上代における社会組織の研究』1921年</ref>。
==首長== 『[[日本書紀]]』の[[神武天皇]]紀には、天皇に服従しない[[紀ノ川]]河口の[[名草戸畔]]（ナグサトベ）、[[熊野]]地方の丹敷戸畔（ニシキトベ）、および[[大和国]]層富県（そほのあがた） の新城戸畔（ニキトベ）という首長の名が残されている。
[[ヤマト王権]]の初期には[[紀国造|木国造]]、すなわち紀伊の首長である[[荒河戸畔|荒河戸辨命]]（アラカワトベ）は子女を[[崇神天皇]]の妃におくり、[[豊城入彦命]]（[[上毛野氏]]および[[下毛野氏]]の祖）や[[豊鍬入姫命]]が生まれている。同時代に[[丹波国]][[氷上郡]]の首長に[[氷香戸辺]]（ヒカトベ）、[[垂仁天皇]]の妃に[[山背国造]]の娘・苅幡戸邊（カリハタトベ）およびに綺戸邊（カニハタトベ）が見える。[[倭建命]]の東征に仕えた[[尾張国造]]の[[建稲種命]]は母に眞敷刀俾（マシキトベ、[[下知我麻神社]]の祭神）、子に尾綱真若刀俾命（オツナマワカトベ）、別名を志理都紀斗売（シリツキトメ）と伝えている。
== トベとトメ == [[作鏡連]]の祖である[[イシコリドメ]]（伊斯許理度売命、石凝姥命）はイシコリ「トベ」（石凝戸辺）とも書かれている。『[[古事記]]』に見られるシリツキトメ（志理都紀斗賣）は、『[[海部氏系図]]』{{efn|偽書とする説がある。}}ではシリツキトベ（志里都岐刀邊）となっている。
[[信濃国]]斗女（トメ）郷は戸辺または富部（トベ）と呼ばれていた<ref> 太田亮 『姓氏家系大辞典』、[[吉田東伍]]『大日本地名辞書」</ref>。また信濃国にはヒカナトメ（[[氷鉋斗賣神社]]）、サラシナトメ（[[更級斗女神社]]）、カワナカジマトメ（[[川中島斗賣神社]]）、[[八坂刀売神|ヤサカトメ]]（[[諏訪大社]]下社）と神名に「トメ」がつく神が多く祀られている。
また『[[先代旧事本紀]]』や『海部氏系図』に現れるアマトメ（天登目命、天斗米命）、タケトメ（建登米命、建斗米命）、タエトメ（妙斗米命）の子孫にムトベ（ 六戸部、六人部、身人部）連が知られており、ここでもトベとトメの関連が見出されるとする説がある。ただし「刀売、斗売、戸弁、戸辺」が[[上代特殊仮名遣い]]の「ト」甲類と「メ・ベ」甲類であるのに対して、「登米・登目」は「ト」乙類と「メ」乙類であり、「斗米」は「ト」甲類と「メ」乙類であるため、首長を表す「トベ・トメ」とは全くの別物である。また六人部の部は[[部民制|部]]の意味であり、六人部も「六」+「人部」ではなく「六人」+「部」である。
==地名のトベ== 現在、[[戸部町 (横浜市)|戸部町]]（[[横浜市]]）や戸部川（[[神奈川県]]）がある。かつて[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛智郡]]には戸部村があり、マシキトベやシリツナマワカトベとの関係が考えられる。[[伊予国]][[伊予郡]]には砥部村→[[砥部町]]がある。
==姓氏のトベ== 現在、戸辺（戸邉・戸邊）姓は[[千葉県]]（特に[[野田市]]）および関東の[[利根川]]沿いに集中している。戸部姓は[[群馬県]]に集中している。富部姓は[[静岡県]]に集中している。土部姓は[[神奈川県]]と[[富山県]]に集中している<ref>「姓名分布＆姓名ランキング」（http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp）にて調査</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm 戸畔考]
{{デフォルトソート:とへ}} [[Category:古代日本の称号]] [[Category:古語]]

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu|qq=}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsuke clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamba Province|qq=}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suinin|qq=}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|qq=}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}, Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|qq=}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}} and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as {{ill|Tobe-chō, Yokohama|ja|戸部町 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobe-chō, Yokohama|qq=}} and Tobe River in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}} retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}}, Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Province|qq=}}, Tobe Village became the modern {{ill|Tobe, Ehime|ja|砥部町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobe, Ehime|qq=}}.
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}, particularly {{ill|Noda|ja|野田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noda|qq=}}. - Tobe (戸部) is common in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}. - Tomibe (富部) is found in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in {{ill|Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}}) used prior to the establishment of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}}. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the {{ill|Kumano Region|ja|熊野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Region|qq=}}, and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu|qq=}}.
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, sent his daughters as consorts to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamba Province|qq=}}, and Emperor {{ill|Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suinin|qq=}}'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|qq=}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).

The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tamba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|qq=}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|qq=}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
