revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Ancient Japanese Titles
revision.text:
'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]) used prior to the establishment of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tamba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|qq=}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|qq=}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]
{{translated page|ja|トベ|version=78810914|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]) used prior to the establishment of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tamba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).

Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."

In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].

Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

<!--Tobe--><!--Tobe--><!--Tobe--><!--Tobe--><!--Tobe--><!--Tobe--> [[Category:Titles of Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Archaic words and phrases]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ceb:Tobe]] [[de:Tobe (Begriffsklärung)]] [[sv:Tobe]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11321375]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]) used prior to the establishment of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tamba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11321375]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]) used prior to the establishment of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tamba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).

In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11321375]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]) used prior to the establishment of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:トベ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11321375]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]) used prior to the establishment of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:トベ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11321375]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]) used prior to the establishment of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:トベ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11321375]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]) used prior to the establishment of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/tobe.htm Analysis of Tobe]

Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:トベ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11321375]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]) used prior to the establishment of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

<!--Tobe--><!--Tobe--><!--Tobe--><!--Tobe--><!--Tobe--><!--Tobe--> [[Category:Titles of Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Archaic words and phrases]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ceb:Tobe]] [[de:Tobe (Begriffsklärung)]] [[sv:Tobe]] [[en:Tobe]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11321375}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:トベ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:トベ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11321375]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]) used prior to the establishment of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).

Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).

Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:トベ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:トベ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:トベ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11321375]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tobe''' (戸畔, 戸弁, 戸辨, 戸辺, 戸邊, 戸邉, 刀俾, 戸部, 富部, 砥部, 土部) was a title (primitive [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]) used prior to the establishment of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]]. {{citation needed span|It later became a common surname and place name.|date=July 2020}} The term "Tobe" is also believed to be the origin of "Tome" (戸賣, 斗女, 刀咩).<ref>Ryō Ōta, ''Study of Social Organization in Ancient Japan,'' 1921.</ref>
== Chieftains == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions chieftains named Nagusa Tobe (名草戸畔) at the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}}, Nishiki Tobe (丹敷戸畔) in the [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano Region]], and Shinjō Tobe (新城戸畔) in the Sōfu County of Yamato Province. These individuals opposed the rule of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]].
During the early Yamato period, Ara-kawa Tobe-no-mikoto (荒河戸辨命), the chieftain of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|lt=Kii clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}}, sent his daughters as consorts to [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], resulting in descendants like {{ill|Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (ancestor of the [[Kamitsukeno clan|Kamitsuke]] and [[Shimotsukeno clan|Shimotsuke clans]]) and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|Toyosukiiri-hime=Toyosukiiri-hime|lt=Toyosukirihime-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}. Other notable Tobe include Hika Tobe (氷香戸辺), a chieftain of [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]]'s consorts, Karibata Tobe (苅幡戸邊) and Kanihata Tobe (綺戸邊).
The Eastern expedition of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was aided by Tobe figures such as Mashiki Tobe (眞敷刀俾), the mother of {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|fr|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}}. Her descendants included Otsuna Mawaka Tobe (尾綱真若刀俾命) and Shiritsuki Tome (志理都紀斗賣).
== Tobe and Tome == The ancestor of the {{ill|Sakakikori|ja|作鏡連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakikori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan, Ishikori Tome (伊斯許理度売命 or 石凝姥命), was also referred to as Ishikori Tobe (石凝戸辺). Similarly, the Shiritsuki Tome mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] is recorded in the {{ill|Ama Clan Genealogy|ja|海部氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan Genealogy|qq=}} as Shiritsuki Tobe (志里都岐刀邊).
In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], Tome settlements, such as {{ill|Tome-go|ja|斗女郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tome-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were also known as Tobe or Tomibe (戸辺, 富部). Several deities with "Tome" in their names are enshrined in Shinano, including Hikana Tome (氷鉋斗賣神社), Sarashina Tome (更級斗女神社), Kawanakajima Tome (川中島斗賣神社), and Yasaka Tome (八坂刀売神社).
The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and the Ama Clan Genealogy list descendants such as Amatome (天登目命), Taketome (建登米命), and Taetome (妙斗米命), related to the Muto Tobe lineage (六戸部, 六人部, 身人部). However, linguistic analyses reveal differences in phonetics, suggesting distinct origins between "Tobe/Tome" and names like "Tomibe."
== Tobe as a Place Name == Modern place names such as [[:en:Tobe, Yokohama|Tobe-chō, Yokohama]] and Tobe River in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] retain the name. In ancient times, Tobe villages existed in [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], Aichi District, linked to Mashiki Tobe. In [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], Tobe Village became the modern [[:en:Tobe, Ehime|Tobe, Ehime]].
== Tobe as a Surname == Today, the Tobe surname appears in various forms: - Tobebe (戸辺, 戸邊) is concentrated in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], particularly [[:en:Noda, Chiba|Noda]]. - Tobe (戸部) is common in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. - Tomibe (富部) is found in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. - Tsuchibe (土部) is concentrated in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]] and [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www2.nipponsoft.co.jp/bldoko/index.asp "Name Distribution and Rankings."]</ref>
